content.principles: Vue3 employs a 'Fast Diff' strategy. 1. Sync head and tail: Identifies and patches identical prefixes and suffixes. 2. Remaining nodes: If nodes remain, it builds a source-to-target index map. 3. LIS Optimization: Computes the Longest Increasing Subsequence of the index map to identify nodes that can stay in place, minimizing move operations. 4. Patch Flags: Leveraging compiler-generated metadata to bypass static nodes.
pitfalls: Using index as 'key' in dynamic lists leads to redundant updates or state bugs.
Component key volatility causing full re-mounts instead of updates.
Deeply nested fragments without stable keys.
follow_up_paths: Vue2 vs Vue3 Diff performance benchmarks
Block Tree and Patch Flags in Vue3 compiler
